"Nihon Kōkogaku Jiten" (Encyclopedia of Japanese Archaeology). Sanseidō, 2003. ISBN 978-4-385-15835-8. * Keiji Nagahara (Supervisor), Eiichi Ishigami, et al. "Iwanami Nihonshi Jiten" (Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary). Iwanami Shoten, 1999. ISBN 978-4-00-080093-8. * ==References== {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|石棺|version=100400894|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Stone coffins]] [[Category:Archaeology of Japan]] [[Category:sekkan]] [[Category:Sekkan]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Sekkan]] [[ja:石棺]] [[ja:日本の石棺]] [[Category:Coffins]] [[Category:Stone (material)]] {{Commons category|Stone coffins}}

{{afc comment|1=Need to find a consistent nomenclature for all of these. Sekkan and Mokka need some level of consistency because of how they are contrasted here * [[Funagata Mokkan]] * [[Funagata Sekkan]] * [[House-shaped Sarcophagus]] * [[Box-shaped stone coffin]] * [[Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus]] * [[Sekkan]] * [[Waritakegata Mokkan]] * [[Waritakegata Sekkan]] * [[Wooden Coffin]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:43, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} '''Stone Coffin''' ({{lang-ja|石棺|1=Sekkan|qq=}}) refers to a coffin made from stone material.[[File:前塚古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|280px|thumb|{{center|Stone coffin excavated from {{ill|Maetsuka Kofun|ja|前塚古墳 (高槻市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maetsuka Kofun|qq=}}}}{{small|On display at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]].}}]] Besides stone coffins, there are also [[:en:Jar_burial|Jar Coffins]], {{ill|Clay Coffins|ja|甕棺|1=Clay coffin chamber|2=ja|3=粘土槨|lt=Clay Coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}}, {{ill|Wooden Coffins|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin|qq=}}, {{ill|Ceramic Coffins|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceramic Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceramic Coffins|qq=}}, and {{ill|Lacquered Coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Kanshitsu coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lacquered Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanshitsu coffin|qq=}}.
Among them, stone coffins are the most durable and airtight, making them suitable for preserving remains. They were crafted and used during the [[:en:Jōmon period|Jōmon period]], [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]], and [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Stone coffins can be classified into smaller box-shaped coffins made by assembling natural flat stones and larger coffins crafted from stone materials.
Box-shaped coffins were also seen on the continent, spreading to eastern Japan in the later [[:en:Jōmon period|Jōmon period]] and arriving in northern Kyushu with {{ill|Rice Cultivation|ja|稲作|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rice Cultivation|qq=}} in the early [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]].
Large stone coffins, though sporadically seen in the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]], are primarily considered a characteristic of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].
Among large stone coffins, there are hollowed-out coffins and assembled ones. Hollowed {{ill|Split-bamboo-shaped Coffins|ja|割竹形石棺|1=Waritakegata Sekkan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Split-bamboo-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Sekkan|qq=}} and {{ill|Boat-shaped Coffins|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boat-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagata Sekkan|qq=}} appeared in the late 4th century.
In the 5th century, {{ill|Long-box-shaped Coffins|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Long-box-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|qq=}} spread mainly in the central Kinki region, while boat-shaped coffins developed in central Kyushu, [[:en:San'in region|San'in Region]], [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku Region]], and [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō Region]].
By the 6th century, unique local hollowed-out and assembled {{ill|House-shaped Coffins|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} were actively used in parts of {{ill|Kinki|ja|近畿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近畿地方}}, [[:en:Kyushu|Kyushu]], [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō Region]], [[:en:San'in region|San'in Region]], [[:en:Tōkai region|Tōkai Region]], and [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō Region]]. Their use continued into the 7th century in some areas. == Stone Coffins in the Jōmon Period == Five stone coffins have been discovered at the Hon-go Yaiseki Ruins in [[:en:Tashiro, Akita|Tashiro, Akita]], [[:en:Kitaakita District, Akita|Kitaakita District, Akita]]. Based on the {{ill|Soil Layer|ja|土層 (考古学)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soil Layer|qq=}} containing the coffins and nearby [[:en:Earthenware|Pottery]], they are estimated to belong to the late Jōmon period. Six coffins have been found at the Kuharayama-toge Ruins in [[:en:Aomori Prefecture|Aomori Prefecture]].斎藤忠『日本考古学用語辞典 改訂新版』学生社 2004th year 248ページ == Stone Coffins in the Yayoi Period == * '''{{ill|Box-shaped Coffins|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-shaped stone coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-shaped stone coffin|qq=}}''' were developed. == Stone Coffins in the Kofun Period == * '''{{ill|Split-bamboo-shaped Coffins|ja|割竹形石棺|1=Waritakegata Sekkan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Split-bamboo-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Sekkan|qq=}}''' (わりだけがたせっかん): Stone coffins modeled after split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffins. * '''{{ill|Boat-shaped Coffins|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boat-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagata Sekkan|qq=}}''' (ふながたせっかん): Both the lid and body were hollowed out, considered a variation of split-bamboo-shaped coffins. * '''{{ill|Long-box-shaped Coffins|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Long-box-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|qq=}}''' (ながもちがたせっかん): Measuring 2–3 meters in length, the body was a box-shaped assembly. * '''{{ill|House-shaped Coffins|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}''' (いえがたせっかん): The body was box-shaped, and the lid resembled a roof. == See Also == * [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] * {{ill|Stone Coffin Tombs in the Yayoi Period|ja|弥生時代の墓制#石棺墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone Coffin Tombs in the Yayoi Period|qq=}} * {{ill|Wooden Coffin Tombs in the Yayoi Period|ja|弥生時代の墓制#木棺墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin Tombs in the Yayoi Period|qq=}} * {{ill|Stone Coffin Chambers|ja|石棺|1=Sekkan|qq=式石室|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone Coffin Chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekkan|qq=}} == References == {{Unreferenced section|section=1|date=2016-03}} * {{ill|Terumi Esaka|ja|江坂輝彌|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Terumi Esaka|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江坂輝弥}}, {{ill|Chōsuke Serizawa|ja|芹沢長介|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōsuke Serizawa|qq=}}, and {{ill|Shūichi Sakazume|ja|坂詰秀一|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shūichi Sakazume|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 坂誥秀一}}, eds. "Shin Nihon Kōkogaku Kōjiten" (New Japanese Archaeological Dictionary). New Science Press, 2005. ISBN 4-8216-0511-2. * Saitō Tadashi. "Nihon Kōkogaku Jiten" (Encyclopedia of Japanese Archaeology). Sanseidō, 2003. ISBN 978-4-385-15835-8. * Keiji Nagahara (Supervisor), Eiichi Ishigami, et al. "Iwanami Nihonshi Jiten" (Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary). Iwanami Shoten, 1999. revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=Need to find a consistent nomenclature for all of these. Sekkan and Mokka need some level of consistency because of how they are contrasted here * [[Funagata Mokkan]] * [[Funagata Sekkan]] * [[House-shaped Sarcophagus]] * [[Box-shaped stone coffin]] * [[Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus]] * [[Sekkan]] * [[Waritakegata Mokkan]] * [[Waritakegata Sekkan]] * [[Wooden Coffin]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:43, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} '''Stone Coffin''' ({{lang-ja|石棺|1=Sekkan|qq=}}) refers to a coffin made from stone material.[[File:前塚古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|280px|thumb|{{center|Stone coffin excavated from {{ill|Maetsuka Kofun|ja|前塚古墳 (高槻市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maetsuka Kofun|qq=}}}}{{small|On display at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]].}}]] Besides stone coffins, there are also [[:en:Jar_burial|Jar Coffins]], {{ill|Clay Coffins|ja|甕棺|1=Clay coffin chamber|2=ja|3=粘土槨|lt=Clay Coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}}, {{ill|Wooden Coffins|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin|1=Wooden Coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin|qq=}}, {{ill|Ceramic Coffins|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceramic Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceramic Coffins|qq=}}, and {{ill|Lacquered Coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Kanshitsu coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lacquered Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanshitsu coffin|qq=}}.
Among them, stone coffins are the most durable and airtight, making them suitable for preserving remains. They were crafted and used during the [[:en:Jōmon period|Jōmon period]], [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]], and [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Stone coffins can be classified into smaller box-shaped coffins made by assembling natural flat stones and larger coffins crafted from stone materials.
Box-shaped coffins were also seen on the continent, spreading to eastern Japan in the later [[:en:Jōmon period|Jōmon period]] and arriving in northern Kyushu with {{ill|Rice Cultivation|ja|稲作|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rice Cultivation|qq=}} in the early [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]].
Large stone coffins, though sporadically seen in the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]], are primarily considered a characteristic of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].
Among large stone coffins, there are hollowed-out coffins and assembled ones. Hollowed {{ill|Split-bamboo-shaped Coffins|ja|割竹形石棺|1=Waritakegata Sekkan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Split-bamboo-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Sekkan|qq=}} and {{ill|Boat-shaped Coffins|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boat-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagata Sekkan|qq=}} appeared in the late 4th century.
In the 5th century, {{ill|Long-box-shaped Coffins|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Long-box-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|qq=}} spread mainly in the central Kinki region, while boat-shaped coffins developed in central Kyushu, [[:en:San'in region|San'in Region]], [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku Region]], and [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō Region]].
By the 6th century, unique local hollowed-out and assembled {{ill|House-shaped Coffins|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} were actively used in parts of {{ill|Kinki|ja|近畿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近畿地方}}, [[:en:Kyushu|Kyushu]], [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō Region]], [[:en:San'in region|San'in Region]], [[:en:Tōkai region|Tōkai Region]], and [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō Region]]. Their use continued into the 7th century in some areas. == Stone Coffins in the Jōmon Period == Five stone coffins have been discovered at the Hon-go Yaiseki Ruins in [[:en:Tashiro, Akita|Tashiro, Akita]], [[:en:Kitaakita District, Akita|Kitaakita District, Akita]]. Based on the {{ill|Soil Layer|ja|土層 (考古学)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soil Layer|qq=}} containing the coffins and nearby [[:en:Earthenware|Pottery]], they are estimated to belong to the late Jōmon period. Six coffins have been found at the Kuharayama-toge Ruins in [[:en:Aomori Prefecture|Aomori Prefecture]].斎藤忠『日本考古学用語辞典 改訂新版』学生社 2004th year 248ページ == Stone Coffins in the Yayoi Period == * '''{{ill|Box-shaped Coffins|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-shaped stone coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-shaped stone coffin|qq=}}''' were developed. == Stone Coffins in the Kofun Period == * '''{{ill|Split-bamboo-shaped Coffins|ja|割竹形石棺|1=Waritakegata Sekkan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Split-bamboo-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Sekkan|qq=}}''' (わりだけがたせっかん): Stone coffins modeled after split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffins. * '''{{ill|Boat-shaped Coffins|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boat-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagata Sekkan|qq=}}''' (ふながたせっかん): Both the lid and body were hollowed out, considered a variation of split-bamboo-shaped coffins. * '''{{ill|Long-box-shaped Coffins|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Long-box-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|qq=}}''' (ながもちがたせっかん): Measuring 2–3 meters in length, the body was a box-shaped assembly. * '''{{ill|House-shaped Coffins|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}''' (いえがたせっかん): The body was box-shaped, and the lid resembled a roof. == See Also == * [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] * {{ill|Stone Coffin Tombs in the Yayoi Period|ja|弥生時代の墓制#石棺墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone Coffin Tombs in the Yayoi Period|qq=}} * {{ill|Wooden Coffin Tombs in the Yayoi Period|ja|弥生時代の墓制#木棺墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin Tombs in the Yayoi Period|qq=}} * {{ill|Stone Coffin Chambers|ja|石棺|1=Sekkan|qq=式石室|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone Coffin Chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekkan|qq=}} == References == {{Unreferenced section|section=1|date=2016-03}} * {{ill|Terumi Esaka|ja|江坂輝彌|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Terumi Esaka|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江坂輝弥}}, {{ill|Chōsuke Serizawa|ja|芹沢長介|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōsuke Serizawa|qq=}}, and {{ill|Shūichi Sakazume|ja|坂詰秀一|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shūichi Sakazume|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 坂誥秀一}}, eds. "Shin Nihon Kōkogaku Kōjiten" (New Japanese Archaeological Dictionary). New Science Press, 2005. ISBN 4-8216-0511-2. * Saitō Tadashi. "Nihon Kōkogaku Yōgo Jiten Kaitei Shinpan" (Dictionary of Japanese Archaeological Terms, Revised Edition). Gakuseisha, 2004. ISBN 978-4-311-75033-5. * Tanaka Taku and Sahara Makoto, eds. "Nihon Kōkogaku Jiten" (Encyclopedia of Japanese Archaeology). Sanseidō, 2003. ISBN 978-4-385-15835-8. * Keiji Nagahara (Supervisor), Eiichi Ishigami, et al. "Iwanami Nihonshi Jiten" (Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary). Iwanami Shoten, 1999. ISBN 978-4-00-080093-8. * ==References== {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|石棺|version=100400894|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Archaeology of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Sekkan]] [[ja:石棺]] [[ja:日本の石棺]] [[Category:Coffins]] [[Category:Stone (material)]] {{Commons category|Stone coffins}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "ox-shaped stone coffin" to "ox-type stone coffin"
revision.text:

Six coffins have been found at the Kuharayama-toge Ruins in [[:en:Aomori Prefecture|Aomori Prefecture]].斎藤忠『日本考古学用語辞典 改訂新版』学生社 2004th year 248ページ == Stone Coffins in the Yayoi Period == * '''{{ill|Box-shaped Coffins|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-type stone coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type stone coffin|qq=}}''' were developed. == Stone Coffins in the Kofun Period == * '''{{ill|Split-bamboo-shaped Coffins|ja|割竹形石棺|1=Waritakegata Sekkan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Split-bamboo-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Sekkan|qq=}}''' (わりだけがたせっかん): Stone coffins modeled after split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffins. * '''{{ill|Boat-shaped Coffins|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boat-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagata Sekkan|qq=}}''' (ふながたせっかん): Both the lid and body were hollowed out, considered a variation of split-bamboo-shaped coffins. * '''{{ill|Long-box-shaped Coffins|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Long-box-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|qq=}}''' (ながもちがたせっかん): Measuring 2–3 meters in length, the body was a box-shaped assembly. * '''{{ill|House-shaped Coffins|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}''' (いえがたせっかん): The body was box-shaped, and the lid resembled a roof. == See Also == * [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] * {{ill|Stone Coffin Tombs in the Yayoi Period|ja|弥生時代の墓制#石棺墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone Coffin Tombs in the Yayoi Period|qq=}} * {{ill|Wooden Coffin Tombs in the Yayoi Period|ja|弥生時代の墓制#木棺墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin Tombs in the Yayoi Period|qq=}} * {{ill|Stone Coffin Chambers|ja|石棺|1=Sekkan|qq=式石室|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone Coffin Chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekkan|qq=}} == References == {{Unreferenced section|section=1|date=2016-03}} * {{ill|Terumi Esaka|ja|江坂輝彌|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Terumi Esaka|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江坂輝弥}}, {{ill|Chōsuke Serizawa|ja|芹沢長介|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōsuke Serizawa|qq=}}, and {{ill|Shūichi Sakazume|ja|坂詰秀一|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shūichi Sakazume|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 坂誥秀一}}, eds. revision.comment: Text replacement - "Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus" to "Chest-shaped stone coffin"
revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11586612]] Wikidata name: stone coffin [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Need to find a consistent nomenclature for all of these. Sekkan and Mokka need some level of consistency because of how they are contrasted here * [[Funagata Mokkan]] * [[Funagata Sekkan]] * [[House-shaped Sarcophagus]] * [[Box-type stone coffin]] * [[Chest-shaped stone coffin]] * [[Sekkan]] * [[Waritakegata Mokkan]] * [[Waritakegata Sekkan]] * [[Wooden Coffin]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:43, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} '''Stone Coffin''' ({{lang-ja|石棺|1=Sekkan|qq=}}) refers to a coffin made from stone material.[[File:前塚古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|280px|thumb|{{center|Stone coffin excavated from {{ill|Maetsuka Kofun|ja|前塚古墳 (高槻市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maetsuka Kofun|qq=}}}}{{small|On display at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]].}}]] Besides stone coffins, there are also [[:en:Jar_burial|Jar Coffins]], {{ill|Clay Coffins|ja|甕棺|1=Clay coffin chamber|2=ja|3=粘土槨|lt=Clay Coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}}, {{ill|Wooden Coffins|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin|1=Wooden Coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin|qq=}}, {{ill|Ceramic Coffins|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceramic Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceramic Coffins|qq=}}, and {{ill|Lacquered Coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Kanshitsu coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lacquered Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanshitsu coffin|qq=}}.
Among them, stone coffins are the most durable and airtight, making them suitable for preserving remains. They were crafted and used during the [[:en:Jōmon period|Jōmon period]], [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]], and [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Stone coffins can be classified into smaller box-shaped coffins made by assembling natural flat stones and larger coffins crafted from stone materials.
Box-shaped coffins were also seen on the continent, spreading to eastern Japan in the later [[:en:Jōmon period|Jōmon period]] and arriving in northern Kyushu with {{ill|Rice Cultivation|ja|稲作|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rice Cultivation|qq=}} in the early [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]].
Large stone coffins, though sporadically seen in the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]], are primarily considered a characteristic of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].
Among large stone coffins, there are hollowed-out coffins and assembled ones. Hollowed {{ill|Split-bamboo-shaped Coffins|ja|割竹形石棺|1=Waritakegata Sekkan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Split-bamboo-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Sekkan|qq=}} and {{ill|Boat-shaped Coffins|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boat-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagata Sekkan|qq=}} appeared in the late 4th century.
In the 5th century, {{ill|Long-box-shaped Coffins|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped stone coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Long-box-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped stone coffin|qq=}} spread mainly in the central Kinki region, while boat-shaped coffins developed in central Kyushu, [[:en:San'in region|San'in Region]], [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku Region]], and [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō Region]].
By the 6th century, unique local hollowed-out and assembled {{ill|House-shaped Coffins|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} were actively used in parts of {{ill|Kinki|ja|近畿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近畿地方}}, [[:en:Kyushu|Kyushu]], [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō Region]], [[:en:San'in region|San'in Region]], [[:en:Tōkai region|Tōkai Region]], and [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō Region]]. Their use continued into the 7th century in some areas. == Stone Coffins in the Jōmon Period == Five stone coffins have been discovered at the Hon-go Yaiseki Ruins in [[:en:Tashiro, Akita|Tashiro, Akita]], [[:en:Kitaakita District, Akita|Kitaakita District, Akita]]. Based on the {{ill|Soil Layer|ja|土層 (考古学)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soil Layer|qq=}} containing the coffins and nearby [[:en:Earthenware|Pottery]], they are estimated to belong to the late Jōmon period. Six coffins have been found at the Kuharayama-toge Ruins in [[:en:Aomori Prefecture|Aomori Prefecture]].斎藤忠『日本考古学用語辞典 改訂新版』学生社 2004th year 248ページ == Stone Coffins in the Yayoi Period == * '''{{ill|Box-shaped Coffins|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-type stone coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type stone coffin|qq=}}''' were developed. == Stone Coffins in the Kofun Period == * '''{{ill|Split-bamboo-shaped Coffins|ja|割竹形石棺|1=Waritakegata Sekkan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Split-bamboo-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Sekkan|qq=}}''' (わりだけがたせっかん): Stone coffins modeled after split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffins. * '''{{ill|Boat-shaped Coffins|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boat-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagata Sekkan|qq=}}''' (ふながたせっかん): Both the lid and body were hollowed out, considered a variation of split-bamboo-shaped coffins. * '''{{ill|Long-box-shaped Coffins|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped stone coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Long-box-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped stone coffin|qq=}}''' (ながもちがたせっかん): Measuring 2–3 meters in length, the body was a box-shaped assembly. * '''{{ill|House-shaped Coffins|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}''' (いえがたせっかん): The body was box-shaped, and the lid resembled a roof. == See Also == * [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] * {{ill|Stone Coffin Tombs in the Yayoi Period|ja|弥生時代の墓制#石棺墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone Coffin Tombs in the Yayoi Period|qq=}} * {{ill|Wooden Coffin Tombs in the Yayoi Period|ja|弥生時代の墓制#木棺墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin Tombs in the Yayoi Period|qq=}} * {{ill|Stone Coffin Chambers|ja|石棺|1=Sekkan|qq=式石室|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone Coffin Chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekkan|qq=}} == References == {{Unreferenced section|section=1|date=2016-03}} * {{ill|Terumi Esaka|ja|江坂輝彌|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Terumi Esaka|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江坂輝弥}}, {{ill|Chōsuke Serizawa|ja|芹沢長介|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōsuke Serizawa|qq=}}, and {{ill|Shūichi Sakazume|ja|坂詰秀一|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shūichi Sakazume|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 坂誥秀一}}, eds. "Shin Nihon Kōkogaku Kōjiten" (New Japanese Archaeological Dictionary). New Science Press, 2005. ISBN 4-8216-0511-2. * Saitō Tadashi. "Nihon Kōkogaku Yōgo Jiten Kaitei Shinpan" (Dictionary of Japanese Archaeological Terms, Revised Edition). Gakuseisha, 2004. ISBN 978-4-311-75033-5. * Tanaka Taku and Sahara Makoto, eds. "Nihon Kōkogaku Jiten" (Encyclopedia of Japanese Archaeology). Sanseidō, 2003. ISBN 978-4-385-15835-8. * Keiji Nagahara (Supervisor), Eiichi Ishigami, et al. "Iwanami Nihonshi Jiten" (Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary). Iwanami Shoten, 1999. ISBN 978-4-00-080093-8. * ==References== {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|石棺|version=100400894|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Archaeology of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Sekkan]] [[ja:石棺]] [[ja:日本の石棺]] [[Category:Coffins]] [[Category:Stone (material)]] {{Commons category|Stone coffins}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11586612]] Wikidata name: stone coffin [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Need to find a consistent nomenclature for all of these. Sekkan and Mokka need some level of consistency because of how they are contrasted here * [[Funagata Mokkan]] * [[Funagata Sekkan]] * [[House-shaped Sarcophagus]] * [[Box-type stone coffin]] * [[Chest-shaped stone coffin]] * [[Sekkan]] * [[Waritakegata Mokkan]] * [[Waritakegata Sekkan]] * [[Wooden Coffin]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:43, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} '''Stone Coffin''' ({{lang-ja|石棺|1=Sekkan|qq=}}) refers to a coffin made from stone material.[[File:前塚古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|280px|thumb|{{center|Stone coffin excavated from {{ill|Maetsuka Kofun|ja|前塚古墳 (高槻市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maetsuka Kofun|qq=}}}}{{small|On display at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]].}}]] Besides stone coffins, there are also [[:en:Jar_burial|Jar Coffins]], {{ill|Clay Coffins|ja|甕棺|1=Clay coffin chamber|2=ja|3=粘土槨|lt=Clay Coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}}, {{ill|Wooden Coffins|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin|1=Wooden Coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin|qq=}}, {{ill|Ceramic Coffins|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceramic Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceramic Coffins|qq=}}, and {{ill|Lacquered Coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Kanshitsu coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lacquered Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanshitsu coffin|qq=}}.
Among them, stone coffins are the most durable and airtight, making them suitable for preserving remains. They were crafted and used during the [[:en:Jōmon period|Jōmon period]], [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]], and [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Stone coffins can be classified into smaller box-shaped coffins made by assembling natural flat stones and larger coffins crafted from stone materials.
Box-shaped coffins were also seen on the continent, spreading to eastern Japan in the later [[:en:Jōmon period|Jōmon period]] and arriving in northern Kyushu with {{ill|Rice Cultivation|ja|稲作|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rice Cultivation|qq=}} in the early [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]].
Large stone coffins, though sporadically seen in the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]], are primarily considered a characteristic of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].
Among large stone coffins, there are hollowed-out coffins and assembled ones. Hollowed {{ill|Split-bamboo-shaped Coffins|ja|割竹形石棺|1=Waritakegata Sekkan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Split-bamboo-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Sekkan|qq=}} and {{ill|Boat-shaped Coffins|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boat-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagata Sekkan|qq=}} appeared in the late 4th century.
In the 5th century, {{ill|Long-box-shaped Coffins|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped stone coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Long-box-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped stone coffin|qq=}} spread mainly in the central Kinki region, while boat-shaped coffins developed in central Kyushu, [[:en:San'in region|San'in Region]], [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku Region]], and [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō Region]].
By the 6th century, unique local hollowed-out and assembled {{ill|House-shaped Coffins|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} were actively used in parts of {{ill|Kinki|ja|近畿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近畿地方|1=Kansai region|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q164256}}, [[:en:Kyushu|Kyushu]], [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō Region]], [[:en:San'in region|San'in Region]], [[:en:Tōkai region|Tōkai Region]], and [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō Region]]. Their use continued into the 7th century in some areas. == Stone Coffins in the Jōmon Period == Five stone coffins have been discovered at the Hon-go Yaiseki Ruins in [[:en:Tashiro, Akita|Tashiro, Akita]], [[:en:Kitaakita District, Akita|Kitaakita District, Akita]]. Based on the {{ill|Soil Layer|ja|土層 (考古学)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soil Layer|qq=|1=Soil Layer|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55524646}} containing the coffins and nearby [[:en:Earthenware|Pottery]], they are estimated to belong to the late Jōmon period. Six coffins have been found at the Kuharayama-toge Ruins in [[:en:Aomori Prefecture|Aomori Prefecture]].斎藤忠『日本考古学用語辞典 改訂新版』学生社 2004th year 248ページ == Stone Coffins in the Yayoi Period == * '''{{ill|Box-shaped Coffins|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-type stone coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type stone coffin|qq=}}''' were developed. == Stone Coffins in the Kofun Period == * '''{{ill|Split-bamboo-shaped Coffins|ja|割竹形石棺|1=Waritakegata Sekkan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Split-bamboo-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Sekkan|qq=}}''' (わりだけがたせっかん): Stone coffins modeled after split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffins. * '''{{ill|Boat-shaped Coffins|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boat-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagata Sekkan|qq=}}''' (ふながたせっかん): Both the lid and body were hollowed out, considered a variation of split-bamboo-shaped coffins. * '''{{ill|Long-box-shaped Coffins|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped stone coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Long-box-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped stone coffin|qq=}}''' (ながもちがたせっかん): Measuring 2–3 meters in length, the body was a box-shaped assembly. * '''{{ill|House-shaped Coffins|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}''' (いえがたせっかん): The body was box-shaped, and the lid resembled a roof. In the 5th century, {{ill|Long-box-shaped Coffins|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped stone coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Long-box-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped stone coffin|qq=}} spread mainly in the central Kinki region, while boat-shaped coffins developed in central Kyushu, [[:en:San'in region|San'in Region]], [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku Region]], and [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō Region]].
By the 6th century, unique local hollowed-out and assembled {{ill|House-shaped Coffins|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} were actively used in parts of {{ill|Kinki|ja|近畿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近畿地方|1=Kansai region|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q164256}}, [[:en:Kyushu|Kyushu]], [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō Region]], [[:en:San'in region|San'in Region]], [[:en:Tōkai region|Tōkai Region]], and [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō Region]]. Their use continued into the 7th century in some areas. == Stone Coffins in the Jōmon Period == Five stone coffins have been discovered at the Hon-go Yaiseki Ruins in [[:en:Tashiro, Akita|Tashiro, Akita]], [[:en:Kitaakita District, Akita|Kitaakita District, Akita]]. Based on the {{ill|Soil Layer|ja|土層 (考古学)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soil Layer|qq=|1=Soil Layer|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55524646}} containing the coffins and nearby [[:en:Earthenware|Pottery]], they are estimated to belong to the late Jōmon period. Six coffins have been found at the Kuharayama-toge Ruins in [[:en:Aomori Prefecture|Aomori Prefecture]].斎藤忠『日本考古学用語辞典 改訂新版』学生社 2004th year 248ページ == Stone Coffins in the Yayoi Period == * '''{{ill|Box-shaped Coffins|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-type stone coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type stone coffin|qq=}}''' were developed. == Stone Coffins in the Kofun Period == * '''{{ill|Split-bamboo-shaped Coffins|ja|割竹形石棺|1=Waritakegata Sekkan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Split-bamboo-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Sekkan|qq=}}''' (わりだけがたせっかん): Stone coffins modeled after split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffins. * '''{{ill|Boat-shaped Coffins|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boat-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagata Sekkan|qq=}}''' (ふながたせっかん): Both the lid and body were hollowed out, considered a variation of split-bamboo-shaped coffins. * '''{{ill|Long-box-shaped Coffins|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped stone coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Long-box-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped stone coffin|qq=}}''' (ながもちがたせっかん): Measuring 2–3 meters in length, the body was a box-shaped assembly. * '''{{ill|House-shaped Coffins|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}''' (いえがたせっかん): The body was box-shaped, and the lid resembled a roof. {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11586612]] Wikidata name: stone coffin [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Need to find a consistent nomenclature for all of these. Sekkan and Mokka need some level of consistency because of how they are contrasted here * [[Funagata Mokkan]] * [[Funagata Sekkan]] * [[House-shaped Sarcophagus]] * [[Box-type stone coffin]] * [[Chest-shaped stone coffin]] * [[Sekkan]] * [[Waritakegata Mokkan]] * [[Waritakegata Sekkan]] * [[Wooden Coffin]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:43, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} '''Stone Coffin''' ({{lang-ja|石棺|1=Sekkan|qq=}}) refers to a coffin made from stone material.[[File:前塚古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|280px|thumb|{{center|Stone coffin excavated from {{ill|Maetsuka Kofun|ja|前塚古墳 (高槻市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maetsuka Kofun|qq=}}}}{{small|On display at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]].}}]] Besides stone coffins, there are also [[:en:Jar_burial|Jar Coffins]], {{ill|Clay Coffins|ja|甕棺|1=Clay coffin chamber|2=ja|3=粘土槨|lt=Clay Coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}}, {{ill|Wooden Coffins|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin|1=Wooden Coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin|qq=}}, {{ill|Ceramic Coffins|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceramic Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceramic Coffins|qq=}}, and {{ill|Lacquered Coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Dry-lacquer coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lacquered Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Dry-lacquer coffin|qq=}}.
Among them, stone coffins are the most durable and airtight, making them suitable for preserving remains. They were crafted and used during the [[:en:Jōmon period|Jōmon period]], [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]], and [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Stone coffins can be classified into smaller box-shaped coffins made by assembling natural flat stones and larger coffins crafted from stone materials.
Box-shaped coffins were also seen on the continent, spreading to eastern Japan in the later [[:en:Jōmon period|Jōmon period]] and arriving in northern Kyushu with {{ill|Rice Cultivation|ja|稲作|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rice Cultivation|qq=}} in the early [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]].
Large stone coffins, though sporadically seen in the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]], are primarily considered a characteristic of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].
Among large stone coffins, there are hollowed-out coffins and assembled ones. Hollowed {{ill|Split-bamboo-shaped Coffins|ja|割竹形石棺|1=Waritakegata Sekkan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Split-bamboo-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Sekkan|qq=}} and {{ill|Boat-shaped Coffins|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boat-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagata Sekkan|qq=}} appeared in the late 4th century.
In the 5th century, {{ill|Long-box-shaped Coffins|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped stone coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Long-box-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped stone coffin|qq=}} spread mainly in the central Kinki region, while boat-shaped coffins developed in central Kyushu, [[:en:San'in region|San'in Region]], [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku Region]], and [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō Region]].
By the 6th century, unique local hollowed-out and assembled {{ill|House-shaped Coffins|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} were actively used in parts of {{ill|Kinki|ja|近畿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近畿地方|1=Kansai region|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q164256}}, [[:en:Kyushu|Kyushu]], [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō Region]], [[:en:San'in region|San'in Region]], [[:en:Tōkai region|Tōkai Region]], and [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō Region]]. Their use continued into the 7th century in some areas. == Stone Coffins in the Jōmon Period == Five stone coffins have been discovered at the Hon-go Yaiseki Ruins in [[:en:Tashiro, Akita|Tashiro, Akita]], [[:en:Kitaakita District, Akita|Kitaakita District, Akita]]. Based on the {{ill|Soil Layer|ja|土層 (考古学)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soil Layer|qq=|1=Soil Layer|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55524646}} containing the coffins and nearby [[:en:Earthenware|Pottery]], they are estimated to belong to the late Jōmon period. Six coffins have been found at the Kuharayama-toge Ruins in [[:en:Aomori Prefecture|Aomori Prefecture]].斎藤忠『日本考古学用語辞典 改訂新版』学生社 2004th year 248ページ == Stone Coffins in the Yayoi Period == * '''{{ill|Box-shaped Coffins|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-type stone coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type stone coffin|qq=}}''' were developed. == Stone Sekkan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boat-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagata Sekkan|qq=}}''' (ふながたせっかん): Both the lid and body were hollowed out, considered a variation of == See Also == * [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] * {{ill|Stone Coffin Tombs in the Yayoi Period|ja|弥生時代の墓制#石棺墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone Coffin Tombs in the Yayoi Period|qq=|1=Funerary practices of the Yayoi period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487466}} * {{ill|Wooden Coffin Tombs in the Yayoi Period|ja|弥生時代の墓制#木棺墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin Tombs in the Yayoi Period|qq=|1=Funerary practices of the Yayoi period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487466}} * {{ill|Stone Coffin Chambers|ja|石棺|1=Sekkan|qq=式石室|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone Coffin revision.comment: Text replacement - "Chest-shaped stone coffin" to "Chest-shaped Sarcophagus"
revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11586612]] Wikidata name: stone coffin [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Need to find a consistent nomenclature for all of these. Sekkan and Mokka need some level of consistency because of how they are contrasted here * [[Funagata Mokkan]] * [[Funagata Sekkan]] * [[House-shaped Sarcophagus]] * [[Box-type stone coffin]] * [[Chest-shaped Sarcophagus]] * [[Sekkan]] * [[Waritakegata Mokkan]] * [[Waritakegata Sekkan]] * [[Wooden Coffin]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:43, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} '''Stone Coffin''' ({{lang-ja|石棺|1=Sekkan|qq=}}) refers to a coffin made from stone material.[[File:前塚古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|280px|thumb|{{center|Stone coffin excavated from {{ill|Maetsuka Kofun|ja|前塚古墳 (高槻市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maetsuka Kofun|qq=}}}}{{small|On display at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]].}}]] Besides stone coffins, there are also [[:en:Jar_burial|Jar Coffins]], {{ill|Clay Coffins|ja|甕棺|1=Clay coffin chamber|2=ja|3=粘土槨|lt=Clay Coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}}, {{ill|Wooden Coffins|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin|1=Wooden Coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin|qq=}}, {{ill|Ceramic Coffins|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceramic Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceramic Coffins|qq=}}, and {{ill|Lacquered Coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Dry-lacquer coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lacquered Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Dry-lacquer coffin|qq=}}.
Among them, stone coffins are the most durable and airtight, making them suitable for preserving remains. They were crafted and used during the [[:en:Jōmon period|Jōmon period]], [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]], and [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Stone coffins can be classified into smaller box-shaped coffins made by assembling natural flat stones and larger coffins crafted from stone materials.
Box-shaped coffins were also seen on the continent, spreading to eastern Japan in the later [[:en:Jōmon period|Jōmon period]] and arriving in northern Kyushu with {{ill|Rice Cultivation|ja|稲作|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rice Cultivation|qq=}} in the early [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]].
Large stone coffins, though sporadically seen in the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]], are primarily considered a characteristic of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].
Among large stone coffins, there are hollowed-out coffins and assembled ones. Hollowed {{ill|Split-bamboo-shaped Coffins|ja|割竹形石棺|1=Waritakegata Sekkan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Split-bamboo-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Sekkan|qq=}} and {{ill|Boat-shaped Coffins|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boat-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagata Sekkan|qq=}} appeared in the late 4th century.
In the 5th century, {{ill|Long-box-shaped Coffins|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Long-box-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} spread mainly in the central Kinki region, while boat-shaped coffins developed in central Kyushu, [[:en:San'in region|San'in Region]], [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku Region]], and [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō Region]].
By the 6th century, unique local hollowed-out and assembled {{ill|House-shaped Coffins|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} were actively used in parts of {{ill|Kinki|ja|近畿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近畿地方|1=Kansai region|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q164256}}, [[:en:Kyushu|Kyushu]], [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō Region]], [[:en:San'in region|San'in Region]], [[:en:Tōkai region|Tōkai Region]], and [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō Region]]. Their use continued into the 7th century in some areas. == Stone Coffins in the Jōmon Period == Five stone coffins have been discovered at the Hon-go Yaiseki Ruins in [[:en:Tashiro, Akita|Tashiro, Akita]], [[:en:Kitaakita District, Akita|Kitaakita District, Akita]]. Based on the {{ill|Soil Layer|ja|土層 (考古学)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soil Layer|qq=|1=Soil Layer|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55524646}} containing the coffins and nearby [[:en:Earthenware|Pottery]], they are estimated to belong to the late Jōmon period. Six coffins have been found at the Kuharayama-toge Ruins in [[:en:Aomori Prefecture|Aomori Prefecture]].斎藤忠『日本考古学用語辞典 改訂新版』学生社 2004th year 248ページ == Stone Coffins in the Yayoi Period == * '''{{ill|Box-shaped Coffins|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-type stone coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type stone coffin|qq=}}''' were developed. == Stone Coffins in the Kofun Period == * '''{{ill|Split-bamboo-shaped Coffins|ja|割竹形石棺|1=Waritakegata Sekkan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Split-bamboo-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Sekkan|qq=}}''' (わりだけがたせっかん): Stone coffins modeled after split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffins. * '''{{ill|Boat-shaped Coffins|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boat-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagata Sekkan|qq=}}''' (ふながたせっかん): Both the lid and body were hollowed out, considered a variation of split-bamboo-shaped coffins. * '''{{ill|Long-box-shaped Coffins|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Long-box-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}''' (ながもちがたせっかん): Measuring 2–3 meters in length, the body was a box-shaped assembly. * '''{{ill|House-shaped Coffins|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}''' (いえがたせっかん): The body was box-shaped, and the lid resembled a roof. == See Also == * [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] * {{ill|Stone Coffin Tombs in the Yayoi Period|ja|弥生時代の墓制#石棺墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone Coffin Tombs in the Yayoi Period|qq=|1=Funerary practices of the Yayoi period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487466}} * {{ill|Wooden Coffin Tombs in the Yayoi Period|ja|弥生時代の墓制#木棺墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin Tombs in the Yayoi Period|qq=|1=Funerary practices of the Yayoi period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487466}} * {{ill|Stone Coffin Chambers|ja|石棺|1=Sekkan|qq=式石室|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone Coffin Chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekkan|qq=}} == References == {{Unreferenced section|section=1|date=2016-03}} * {{ill|Terumi Esaka|ja|江坂輝彌|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Terumi Esaka|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江坂輝弥|1=Teruya Esaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11550799}}, {{ill|Chōsuke Serizawa|ja|芹沢長介|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōsuke Serizawa|qq=|1=Serizawa Chōsuke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616035}}, and {{ill|Shūichi Sakazume|ja|坂詰秀一|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shūichi Sakazume|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 坂誥秀一|1=Hideichi Sakazume|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11425571}}, eds. "Shin Nihon Kōkogaku Kōjiten" (New Japanese Archaeological Dictionary). New Science Press, 2005. ISBN 4-8216-0511-2. * Saitō Tadashi. "Nihon Kōkogaku Yōgo Jiten Kaitei Shinpan" (Dictionary of Japanese Archaeological Terms, Revised Edition). Gakuseisha, 2004. ISBN 978-4-311-75033-5. * Tanaka Taku and Sahara Makoto, eds. "Nihon Kōkogaku Jiten" (Encyclopedia of Japanese Archaeology). Sanseidō, 2003. ISBN 978-4-385-15835-8. * Keiji Nagahara (Supervisor), Eiichi Ishigami, et al. "Iwanami Nihonshi Jiten" (Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary). Iwanami Shoten, 1999. ISBN 978-4-00-080093-8. * ==References== {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|石棺|version=100400894|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Archaeology of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:石棺]] [[ja:日本の石棺]] [[Category:Coffins]] [[Category:Stone (material)]] [[en:Sekkan]] {{Commons category|Stone coffins}} {{wikidata link|Q11586612}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11586612]] Wikidata name: stone coffin [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Need to find a consistent nomenclature for all of these. Sekkan and Mokka need some level of consistency because of how they are contrasted here * [[Funagata Mokkan]] * [[Funagata Sekkan]] * [[House-shaped Sarcophagus]] * [[Box-type stone coffin]] * [[Chest-shaped Sarcophagus]] * [[Sekkan]] * [[Waritakegata Mokkan]] * [[Waritakegata Sekkan]] * [[Wooden Coffin]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:43, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} '''Stone Coffin''' ({{lang-ja|石棺|1=Sekkan|1=Sekkan (Sarcophagus)|lt=Sarcophagus|qq=}}) refers to a coffin made from stone material.[[File:前塚古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|280px|thumb|{{center|Stone coffin excavated from {{ill|Maetsuka Kofun|ja|前塚古墳 (高槻市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maetsuka Kofun|qq=}}}}{{small|On display at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]].}}]] Besides stone coffins, there are also [[:en:Jar_burial|Jar Coffins]], {{ill|Clay Coffins|ja|甕棺|1=Clay coffin chamber|2=ja|3=粘土槨|lt=Clay Coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}}, {{ill|Wooden Coffins|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin|1=Wooden Coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin|qq=}}, {{ill|Ceramic Coffins|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceramic Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceramic Coffins|qq=}}, and {{ill|Lacquered Coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Dry-lacquer coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lacquered Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Dry-lacquer coffin|qq=}}.
Among them, stone coffins are the most durable and airtight, making them suitable for preserving remains. They were crafted and used during the [[:en:Jōmon period|Jōmon period]], [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]], and [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Stone coffins can be classified into smaller box-shaped coffins made by assembling natural flat stones and larger coffins crafted from stone materials.
Box-shaped coffins were also seen on the continent, spreading to eastern Japan in the later [[:en:Jōmon period|Jōmon period]] and arriving in northern Kyushu with {{ill|Rice Cultivation|ja|稲作|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rice Cultivation|qq=}} in the early [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]].
Large stone coffins, though sporadically seen in the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]], are primarily considered a characteristic of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].
Among large stone coffins, there are hollowed-out coffins and assembled ones. Hollowed {{ill|Split-bamboo-shaped Coffins|ja|割竹形石棺|1=Waritakegata Sekkan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Split-bamboo-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Sekkan|qq=}} and {{ill|Boat-shaped Coffins|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boat-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagata Sekkan|qq=}} appeared in the late 4th century.
In the 5th century, {{ill|Long-box-shaped Coffins|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Long-box-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} spread mainly in the central Kinki region, while boat-shaped coffins developed in central Kyushu, [[:en:San'in region|San'in Region]], [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku Region]], and [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō Region]].
By the 6th century, unique local hollowed-out and assembled {{ill|House-shaped Coffins|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} were actively used in parts of {{ill|Kinki|ja|近畿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近畿地方|1=Kansai region|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q164256}}, [[:en:Kyushu|Kyushu]], [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō Region]], [[:en:San'in region|San'in Region]], [[:en:Tōkai region|Tōkai Region]], and [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō Region]]. Their use continued into the 7th century in some areas. == Stone Coffins in the Jōmon Period == Five stone coffins have been discovered at the Hon-go Yaiseki Ruins in [[:en:Tashiro, Akita|Tashiro, Akita]], [[:en:Kitaakita District, Akita|Kitaakita District, Akita]]. Based on the {{ill|Soil Layer|ja|土層 (考古学)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soil Layer|qq=|1=Soil Layer|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55524646}} containing the coffins and nearby [[:en:Earthenware|Pottery]], they are estimated to belong to the late Jōmon period. Six coffins have been found at the Kuharayama-toge Ruins in [[:en:Aomori Prefecture|Aomori Prefecture]].斎藤忠『日本考古学用語辞典 改訂新版』学生社 2004th year 248ページ == Stone Coffins in the Yayoi Period == * '''{{ill|Box-shaped Coffins|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-type stone coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type stone coffin|qq=}}''' were developed. == Stone Coffins in the Kofun Period == * '''{{ill|Split-bamboo-shaped Coffins|ja|割竹形石棺|1=Waritakegata Sekkan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Split-bamboo-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Sekkan|qq=}}''' (わりだけがたせっかん): Stone coffins modeled after split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffins. * '''{{ill|Boat-shaped Coffins|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boat-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagata Sekkan|qq=}}''' (ふながたせっかん): Both the lid and body were hollowed out, considered a variation of split-bamboo-shaped coffins. * '''{{ill|Long-box-shaped Coffins|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Long-box-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}''' (ながもちがたせっかん): Measuring 2–3 meters in length, the body was a box-shaped assembly. * '''{{ill|House-shaped Coffins|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}''' (いえがたせっかん): The body was box-shaped, and the lid resembled a roof. In the 5th century, {{ill|Long-box-shaped Coffins|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Long-box-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} spread mainly in the central Kinki region, while boat-shaped coffins developed in central Kyushu, [[:en:San'in region|San'in Region]], [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku Region]], and [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō Region]].
By the 6th century, unique local hollowed-out and assembled {{ill|House-shaped Coffins|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} were actively used in parts of {{ill|Kinki|ja|近畿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近畿地方|1=Kansai region|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q164256}}, [[:en:Kyushu|Kyushu]], [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō Region]], [[:en:San'in region|San'in Region]], [[:en:Tōkai region|Tōkai Region]], and [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō Region]]. Their use continued into the 7th century in some areas. == Stone Coffins in the Jōmon Period == Five stone coffins have been discovered at the Hon-go Yaiseki Ruins in [[:en:Tashiro, Akita|Tashiro, Akita]], [[:en:Kitaakita District, Akita|Kitaakita District, Akita]]. Based on the {{ill|Soil Layer|ja|土層 (考古学)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soil Layer|qq=|1=Soil Layer|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55524646}} containing the coffins and nearby [[:en:Earthenware|Pottery]], they are estimated to belong to the late Jōmon period. Six coffins have been found at the Kuharayama-toge Ruins in [[:en:Aomori Prefecture|Aomori Prefecture]].斎藤忠『日本考古学用語辞典 改訂新版』学生社 2004th year 248ページ == Stone Coffins in the Yayoi Period == * Sekkan and Mokka need some level of consistency because of how they are contrasted here * [[Funagata Mokkan]] * [[Funagata Sarcophagus]] * [[House-shaped Sarcophagus]] * [[Box-type stone coffin]] * [[Chest-shaped Sarcophagus]] * [[Sekkan]] * [[Waritakegata Mokkan]] * [[Waritakegata Sarcophagus]] * [[Wooden Coffin]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:43, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Nihongo|Sekkan|石棺}} refers to a coffin made from stone material.[[File:前塚古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|280px|thumb|{{center|Stone coffin excavated from {{ill|Maetsuka Kofun|ja|前塚古墳 (高槻市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maetsuka Kofun|qq=}}}}{{small|On display at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]].}}]] Besides stone coffins, there are also [[:en:Jar_burial|Jar Coffins]], {{ill|Clay Coffins|ja|甕棺|1=Clay coffin chamber|2=ja|3=粘土槨|lt=Clay Coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}}, {{ill|Wooden Coffins|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin|1=Wooden Coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin|qq=}}, {{ill|Ceramic Coffins|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceramic Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceramic Coffins|qq=}}, and {{ill|Lacquered Coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Dry-lacquer coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lacquered Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Dry-lacquer coffin|qq=}}.
Among them, stone coffins are the most durable and airtight, making them suitable for preserving remains. They were crafted and used during the [[:en:Jōmon period|Jōmon period]], [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]], and [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Stone coffins can be classified into smaller box-shaped coffins made by assembling natural flat stones and larger coffins crafted from stone materials.
Box-shaped coffins were also seen on the continent, spreading to eastern Japan in the later [[:en:Jōmon period|Jōmon period]] and arriving in northern Kyushu with {{ill|Rice Cultivation|ja|稲作|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rice Cultivation|qq=}} in the early [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]].
Large stone coffins, though sporadically seen in the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]], are primarily considered a characteristic of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].
Among large stone coffins, there are hollowed-out coffins and assembled ones. Hollowed {{ill|Split-bamboo-shaped Coffins|ja|割竹形石棺|1=Waritakegata Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Split-bamboo-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Sarcophagus|qq=}} and {{ill|Boat-shaped Coffins|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boat-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagata Sarcophagus|qq=}} appeared in the late 4th century.
In the 5th century, {{ill|Long-box-shaped Coffins|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Long-box-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} spread mainly in the central Kinki region, while boat-shaped coffins developed in central Kyushu, [[:en:San'in region|San'in Region]], [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku Region]], and [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō Region]].
By the 6th century, unique local hollowed-out and assembled {{ill|House-shaped Coffins|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} were actively used in parts of {{ill|Kinki|ja|近畿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近畿地方|1=Kansai region|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q164256}}, [[:en:Kyushu|Kyushu]], [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō Region]], [[:en:San'in region|San'in Region]], [[:en:Tōkai region|Tōkai Region]], and [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō Region]]. Their use continued into the 7th century in some areas. == Stone Coffins in the Jōmon Period == Five stone coffins have been discovered at the Hon-go Yaiseki Ruins in [[:en:Tashiro, Akita|Tashiro, Akita]], [[:en:Kitaakita District, Akita|Kitaakita District, Akita]]. Based on the {{ill|Soil Layer|ja|土層 (考古学)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soil Layer|qq=|1=Soil Layer|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55524646}} containing the coffins and nearby [[:en:Earthenware|Pottery]], they are estimated to belong to the late Jōmon period. Six coffins have been found at the Kuharayama-toge Ruins in [[:en:Aomori Prefecture|Aomori Prefecture]].斎藤忠『日本考古学用語辞典 改訂新版』学生社 2004th year 248ページ == Stone Coffins in the Yayoi Period == * {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11586612]] Wikidata name: Sarcophagus [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Need to find a consistent nomenclature for all of these. Sekkan and Mokka need some level of consistency because of how they are contrasted here * [[Funagata Mokkan]] * [[Funagata Sarcophagus]] * [[House-shaped Sarcophagus]] * [[Box-type Sarcophagus]] * [[Chest-shaped Sarcophagus]] * [[Sekkan]] * [[Waritakegata Mokkan]] * [[Waritakegata Sarcophagus]] * [[Wooden Coffin]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:43, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Nihongo|Sekkan|石棺}} refers to a coffin made from stone material.[[File:前塚古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|280px|thumb|{{center|Sarcophagus excavated from {{ill|Maetsuka Kofun|ja|前塚古墳 (高槻市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maetsuka Kofun|qq=}}}}{{small|On display at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]].}}]] Besides Sarcophaguss, there are also [[:en:Jar_burial|Jar Coffins]], {{ill|Clay Coffins|ja|甕棺|1=Clay coffin chamber|2=ja|3=粘土槨|lt=Clay Coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}}, {{ill|Wooden Coffins|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin|1=Wooden Coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin|qq=}}, {{ill|Ceramic Coffins|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceramic Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceramic Coffins|qq=}}, and {{ill|Lacquered Coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Dry-lacquer coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lacquered Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Dry-lacquer coffin|qq=}}.
Among them, Sarcophaguss are the most durable and airtight, making them suitable for preserving remains. They were crafted and used during the [[:en:Jōmon period|Jōmon period]], [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]], and [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Sarcophaguss can be classified into smaller box-shaped coffins made by assembling natural flat stones and larger coffins crafted from stone materials.
Box-shaped coffins were also seen on the continent, spreading to eastern Japan in the later [[:en:Jōmon period|Jōmon period]] and arriving in northern Kyushu with {{ill|Rice Cultivation|ja|稲作|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rice Cultivation|qq=}} in the early [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]].
Large Sarcophaguss, though sporadically seen in the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]], are primarily considered a characteristic of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].
Among large Sarcophaguss, there are hollowed-out coffins and assembled ones. Hollowed {{ill|Split-bamboo-shaped Coffins|ja|割竹形石棺|1=Waritakegata Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Split-bamboo-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Sarcophagus|qq=}} and {{ill|Boat-shaped Coffins|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boat-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagata Sarcophagus|qq=}} appeared in the late 4th century.
In the 5th century, {{ill|Long-box-shaped Coffins|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Long-box-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} spread mainly in the central Kinki region, while boat-shaped coffins developed in central Kyushu, [[:en:San'in region|San'in Region]], [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku Region]], and [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō Region]].
By the 6th century, unique local hollowed-out and assembled {{ill|House-shaped Coffins|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} were actively used in parts of {{ill|Kinki|ja|近畿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近畿地方|1=Kansai region|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q164256}}, [[:en:Kyushu|Kyushu]], [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō Region]], [[:en:San'in region|San'in Region]], [[:en:Tōkai region|Tōkai Region]], and [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō Region]]. Their use continued into the 7th century in some areas. == Stone Coffins in the Jōmon Period == Five Sarcophaguss have been discovered at the Hon-go Yaiseki Ruins in [[:en:Tashiro, Akita|Tashiro, Akita]], [[:en:Kitaakita District, Akita|Kitaakita District, Akita]]. Based on the {{ill|Soil Layer|ja|土層 (考古学)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soil Layer|qq=|1=Soil Layer|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55524646}} containing the coffins and nearby [[:en:Earthenware|Pottery]], they are estimated to belong to the late Jōmon period. Six coffins have been found at the Kuharayama-toge Ruins in [[:en:Aomori Prefecture|Aomori Prefecture]].斎藤忠『日本考古学用語辞典 改訂新版』学生社 2004th year 248ページ == Stone Coffins in the Yayoi Period == * '''{{ill|Box-shaped Coffins|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-type Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type Sarcophagus|qq=}}''' were developed. == Stone Coffins in the Kofun Period == * '''{{ill|Split-bamboo-shaped Coffins|ja|割竹形石棺|1=Waritakegata Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Split-bamboo-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Sarcophagus|qq=}}''' (わりだけがたせっかん): Sarcophaguss modeled after split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffins. * '''{{ill|Boat-shaped Coffins|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boat-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagata Sarcophagus|qq=}}''' (ふながたせっかん): Both the lid and body were hollowed out, considered a variation of split-bamboo-shaped coffins. * '''{{ill|Long-box-shaped Coffins|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Long-box-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}''' (ながもちがたせっかん): Measuring 2–3 meters in length, the body was a box-shaped assembly. * '''{{ill|House-shaped Coffins|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}''' (いえがたせっかん): The body was box-shaped, and the lid resembled a roof. Sekkan and Mokka need some level of consistency because of how they are contrasted here * [[Funagata Mokkan]] * [[Funagata Sarcophagus]] * [[House-shaped Sarcophagus]] * [[Box-type Sarcophagus]] * [[Chest-shaped Sarcophagus]] * [[Sekkan]] * [[Waritakegata Mokkan]] * [[Waritakegata Sarcophagus]] * [[Wooden Coffin]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:43, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Nihongo|Sekkan|石棺}} refers to a coffin made from stone material.[[File:前塚古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|280px|thumb|{{center|Sarcophagus excavated from {{ill|Maetsuka Kofun|ja|前塚古墳 (高槻市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maetsuka Kofun|qq=}}}}{{small|On display at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]].}}]] Besides Sarcophaguss, there are also [[:en:Jar_burial|Jar Coffins]], {{ill|Clay Coffins|ja|甕棺|1=Clay coffin chamber|2=ja|3=粘土槨|lt=Clay Coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}}, {{ill|Wooden Coffins|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin|1=Wooden Coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin|qq=}}, {{ill|Ceramic Coffins|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceramic Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceramic Coffins|qq=}}, and {{ill|Lacquered Coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Dry-lacquer coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lacquered Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Dry-lacquer coffin|qq=}}.
Among them, Sarcophaguss are the most durable and airtight, making them suitable for preserving remains. They were crafted and used during the [[:en:Jōmon period|Jōmon period]], [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]], and [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Sarcophaguss can be classified into smaller box-shaped coffins made by assembling natural flat stones and larger coffins crafted from stone materials.
Box-shaped coffins were also seen on the continent, spreading to eastern Japan in the later [[:en:Jōmon period|Jōmon period]] and arriving in northern Kyushu with {{ill|Rice Cultivation|ja|稲作|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rice Cultivation|qq=}} in the early [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]].
Large Sarcophaguss, though sporadically seen in the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]], are primarily considered a characteristic of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].
Among large Sarcophaguss, there are hollowed-out coffins and assembled ones. Hollowed {{ill|Split-bamboo-shaped Coffins|ja|割竹形石棺|1=Waritakegata Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Split-bamboo-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Sarcophagus|qq=}} and {{ill|Boat-shaped Coffins|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boat-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagata Sarcophagus|qq=}} appeared in the late 4th century.
In the 5th century, {{ill|Long-box-shaped Coffins|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Long-box-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} spread mainly in the central Kinki region, while boat-shaped coffins developed in central Kyushu, [[:en:San'in region|San'in Region]], [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku Region]], and [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō Region]].
By the 6th century, unique local hollowed-out and assembled {{ill|House-shaped Coffins|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} were actively used in parts of {{ill|Kinki|ja|近畿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近畿地方|1=Kansai region|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q164256}}, [[:en:Kyushu|Kyushu]], [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō Region]], [[:en:San'in region|San'in Region]], [[:en:Tōkai region|Tōkai Region]], and [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō Region]]. Their use continued into the 7th century in some areas. == Stone Coffins in the Jōmon Period == Five Sarcophaguss have been discovered at the Hon-go Yaiseki Ruins in [[:en:Tashiro, Akita|Tashiro, Akita]], [[:en:Kitaakita District, Akita|Kitaakita District, Akita]]. Based on the {{ill|Soil Layer|ja|土層 (考古学)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soil Layer|qq=|1=Soil Layer|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55524646}} containing the coffins and nearby [[:en:Earthenware|Pottery]], they are estimated to belong to the late Jōmon period. Six coffins have been found at the Kuharayama-toge Ruins in [[:en:Aomori Prefecture|Aomori Prefecture]].斎藤忠『日本考古学用語辞典 改訂新版』学生社 2004th year 248ページ == Stone Coffins in the Yayoi Period == * '''{{ill|Box-shaped Coffins|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-type Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type Sarcophagus|qq=}}''' were developed. == Stone Coffins in the Kofun Period == * '''{{ill|Split-bamboo-shaped Coffins|ja|割竹形石棺|1=Waritakegata Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Split-bamboo-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Sarcophagus|qq=}}''' (わりだけがたせっかん): Sarcophaguss modeled after split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffins. * '''{{ill|Boat-shaped Coffins|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boat-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagata Sarcophagus|qq=}}''' (ふながたせっかん): Both the lid and body were hollowed out, considered a variation of split-bamboo-shaped coffins. * '''{{ill|Long-box-shaped Coffins|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Long-box-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}''' (ながもちがたせっかん): Measuring 2–3 meters in length, the body was a box-shaped assembly. * '''{{ill|House-shaped Coffins|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}''' (いえがたせっかん): The body was box-shaped, and the lid resembled a roof. In the 5th century, {{ill|Long-box-shaped Coffins|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Long-box-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} spread mainly in the central Kinki region, while boat-shaped coffins developed in central Kyushu, [[:en:San'in region|San'in Region]], [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku Region]], and [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō Region]].
By the 6th century, unique local hollowed-out and assembled {{ill|House-shaped Coffins|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} were actively used in parts of {{ill|Kinki|ja|近畿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近畿地方|1=Kansai region|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q164256}}, [[:en:Kyushu|Kyushu]], [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō Region]], [[:en:San'in region|San'in Region]], [[:en:Tōkai region|Tōkai Region]], and [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō Region]]. Their use continued into the 7th century in some areas. == Stone Coffins in the Jōmon Period == Five Sarcophaguss have been discovered at the Hon-go Yaiseki Ruins in [[:en:Tashiro, Akita|Tashiro, Akita]], [[:en:Kitaakita District, Akita|Kitaakita District, Akita]]. Based on the {{ill|Soil Layer|ja|土層 (考古学)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soil Layer|1=Soil Layer|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55524646}} containing the coffins and nearby [[:en:Earthenware|Pottery]], they are estimated to belong to the late Jōmon period. Six coffins have been found at the Kuharayama-toge Ruins in [[:en:Aomori Prefecture|Aomori Prefecture]].斎藤忠『日本考古学用語辞典 改訂新版』学生社 2004th year 248ページ == Stone Coffins in the Yayoi Period == *
