revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Stone coffins]][[ja:石棺]] [[co:commons:Category:Stone coffins]][[ja:石棺]] [[sn:commons:Category:Stone coffins]][[ja:石棺]] {{Nihongo|Sekkan|石棺}} refers to a coffin made from stone material.[[File:前塚古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|280px|thumb|{{center|Sarcophagus excavated from {{ill|Maetsuka Kofun|ja|前塚古墳 (高槻市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maetsuka Kofun|qq=}}}}{{small|On display at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|en|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|WD=Q4557237|lt=Chikatsu Asuka Museum|1=Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum}}.}}]] Besides Sarcophaguss, there are also {{ill|Jar_burial|en|Jar_burial|WD=Q4244233|lt=Jar Coffins|1=Jar burial}}, {{ill|Clay Coffins|ja|甕棺|1=Clay coffin chamber|2=ja|3=粘土槨|lt=Clay Coffin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}}, {{ill|Wooden Coffins|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin|1=Wooden Coffin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin|qq=}}, {{ill|Ceramic Coffins|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceramic Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceramic Coffins|qq=}}, and {{ill|Lacquered Coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Dry-lacquer coffin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lacquered Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Dry-lacquer coffin|qq=}}.
Among them, Sarcophaguss are the most durable and airtight, making them suitable for preserving remains. They were crafted and used during the {{ill|Jōmon period|en|Jōmon period|WD=Q52824|lt=Jōmon period|1=Jōmon period}}, {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period|1=Yayoi period}}, and {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}. Sarcophaguss can be classified into smaller box-shaped coffins made by assembling natural flat stones and larger coffins crafted from stone materials.
Box-shaped coffins were also seen on the continent, spreading to eastern Japan in the later {{ill|Jōmon period|en|Jōmon period|WD=Q52824|lt=Jōmon period|1=Jōmon period}} and arriving in northern Kyushu with {{ill|Rice Cultivation|ja|稲作|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rice Cultivation|qq=}} in the early {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period|1=Yayoi period}}.
Large Sarcophaguss, though sporadically seen in the {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period|1=Yayoi period}}, are primarily considered a characteristic of the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}.
Among large Sarcophaguss, there are hollowed-out coffins and assembled ones. Hollowed {{ill|Split-bamboo-shaped Coffins|ja|割竹形石棺|1=Waritakegata Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Split-bamboo-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Sarcophagus|qq=}} and {{ill|Boat-shaped Coffins|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boat-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagata Sarcophagus|qq=}} appeared in the late 4th century.
In the 5th century, {{ill|Long-box-shaped Coffins|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Long-box-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} spread mainly in the central Kinki region, while boat-shaped coffins developed in central Kyushu, {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|WD=Q398310|lt=San'in Region|1=San'in region}}, {{ill|Hokuriku region|en|Hokuriku region|WD=Q381025|lt=Hokuriku Region|1=Hokuriku region}}, and {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō Region|1=Kantō region}}.
By the 6th century, unique local hollowed-out and assembled {{ill|House-shaped Coffins|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} were actively used in parts of {{ill|Kinki|ja|近畿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近畿地方|1=Kansai region|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q164256}}, {{ill|Kyushu|en|Kyushu|WD=Q13987|lt=Kyushu|1=Kyushu}}, {{ill|San'yō region|en|San'yō region|WD=Q398087|lt=San'yō Region|1=San'yō region}}, {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|WD=Q398310|lt=San'in Region|1=San'in region}}, {{ill|Tōkai region|en|Tōkai region|WD=Q398989|lt=Tōkai Region|1=Tōkai region}}, and {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō Region|1=Kantō region}}. Their use continued into the 7th century in some areas. == Stone Coffins in the Jōmon Period == Five Sarcophaguss have been discovered at the Hon-go Yaiseki Ruins in {{ill|Tashiro, Akita|en|Tashiro, Akita|WD=Q7687213|lt=Tashiro, Akita|1=Tashiro, Akita}}, {{ill|Kitaakita District, Akita|en|Kitaakita District, Akita|WD=Q1208682|lt=Kitaakita District, Akita|1=Kitaakita District, Akita}}. Based on the {{ill|Soil Layer|ja|土層 (考古学)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soil Layer|1=Q55524646|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55524646}} containing the coffins and nearby {{ill|Earthenware|en|Earthenware|WD=Q1377111|lt=Pottery|1=Earthenware}}, they are estimated to belong to the late Jōmon period. Six coffins have been found at the Kuharayama-toge Ruins in {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|en|Aomori Prefecture|WD=Q71699|lt=Aomori Prefecture|1=Aomori Prefecture}}.斎藤忠『日本考古学用語辞典 改訂新版』学生社 2004th year 248ページ == Stone Coffins in the Yayoi Period == * '''{{ill|Box-shaped Coffins|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-type Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type Sarcophagus|qq=}}''' were developed. == Stone Coffins in the Kofun Period == * '''{{ill|Split-bamboo-shaped Coffins|ja|割竹形石棺|1=Waritakegata Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Split-bamboo-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Sarcophagus|qq=}}''' (わりだけがたせっかん): Sarcophaguss modeled after split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffins. * '''{{ill|Boat-shaped Coffins|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boat-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagata Sarcophagus|qq=}}''' (ふながたせっかん): Both the lid and body were hollowed out, considered a variation of split-bamboo-shaped coffins. * '''{{ill|Long-box-shaped Coffins|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Long-box-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}''' (ながもちがたせっかん): Measuring 2–3 meters in length, the body was a box-shaped assembly. * '''{{ill|House-shaped Coffins|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}''' (いえがたせっかん): The body was box-shaped, and the lid resembled a roof. == See Also == * {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} * {{ill|Stone Coffin Tombs in the Yayoi Period|ja|弥生時代の墓制#石棺墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone Coffin Tombs in the Yayoi Period|1=Funerary practices of the Yayoi period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487466}} * {{ill|Wooden Coffin Tombs in the Yayoi Period|ja|弥生時代の墓制#木棺墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin Tombs in the Yayoi Period|1=Funerary practices of the Yayoi period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487466}} * {{ill|Stone Coffin Chambers|ja|石棺|1=Sekkan|1=Sekkan (Sarcophagus)|lt=Sarcophagus|qq=式石室|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone Coffin Chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekkan|qq=}} == References == {{Unreferenced section|section=1|date=2016-03}} * {{ill|Terumi Esaka|ja|江坂輝彌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Terumi Esaka|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江坂輝弥|1=Teruya Esaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11550799}}, {{ill|Chōsuke Serizawa|ja|芹沢長介|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōsuke Serizawa|1=Serizawa Chōsuke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616035}}, and {{ill|Shūichi Sakazume|ja|坂詰秀一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shūichi Sakazume|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 坂誥秀一|1=Hideichi Sakazume|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11425571}}, eds. "Shin Nihon Kōkogaku Kōjiten" (New Japanese Archaeological Dictionary). New Science Press, 2005. ISBN 4-8216-0511-2. * Saitō Tadashi. "Nihon Kōkogaku Yōgo Jiten Kaitei Shinpan" (Dictionary of Japanese Archaeological Terms, Revised Edition). Gakuseisha, 2004. ISBN 978-4-311-75033-5. * Tanaka Taku and Sahara Makoto, eds. "Nihon Kōkogaku Jiten" (Encyclopedia of Japanese Archaeology). Sanseidō, 2003. ISBN 978-4-385-15835-8. * Keiji Nagahara (Supervisor), Eiichi Ishigami, et al. "Iwanami Nihonshi Jiten" (Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary). Iwanami Shoten, 1999. ISBN 978-4-00-080093-8. * ==References== {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|石棺|version=100400894|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:石棺]] [[ja:日本の石棺]] [[en:Sekkan]] {{Commons category|Sarcophaguss}} {{wikidata link|Q11586612}} [[Category:Archaeology of Japan]] [[Category:Coffins]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Stone (material)]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]

[[co:commons:Category:Stone coffins]][[ja:石棺]] [[co:commons:Category:Stone coffins]][[ja:石棺]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Stone coffins]][[ja:石棺]] {{Nihongo|Sekkan|石棺}} refers to a coffin made from stone material.[[File:前塚古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|280px|thumb|{{center|Sarcophagus excavated from {{ill|Maetsuka Kofun|ja|前塚古墳 (高槻市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maetsuka Kofun|qq=}}}}{{small|On display at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|en|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|WD=Q4557237|lt=Chikatsu Asuka Museum|1=Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum}}.}}]] Besides Sarcophaguss, there are also {{ill|Jar_burial|en|Jar_burial|WD=Q4244233|lt=Jar Coffins|1=Jar burial}}, {{ill|Clay Coffins|ja|甕棺|1=Clay coffin chamber|2=ja|3=粘土槨|lt=Clay Coffin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}}, {{ill|Wooden Coffins|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin|1=Wooden Coffin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin|qq=}}, {{ill|Ceramic Coffins|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceramic Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceramic Coffins|qq=}}, and {{ill|Lacquered Coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Dry-lacquer coffin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lacquered Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Dry-lacquer coffin|qq=}}.
Among them, Sarcophaguss are the most durable and airtight, making them suitable for preserving remains. They were crafted and used during the {{ill|Jōmon period|en|Jōmon period|WD=Q52824|lt=Jōmon period|1=Jōmon period}}, {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period|1=Yayoi period}}, and {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}. Sarcophaguss can be classified into smaller box-shaped coffins made by assembling natural flat stones and larger coffins crafted from stone materials.
Box-shaped coffins were also seen on the continent, spreading to eastern Japan in the later {{ill|Jōmon period|en|Jōmon period|WD=Q52824|lt=Jōmon period|1=Jōmon period}} and arriving in northern Kyushu with {{ill|Rice Cultivation|ja|稲作|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rice Cultivation|qq=}} in the early {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period|1=Yayoi period}}.
Large Sarcophaguss, though sporadically seen in the {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period|1=Yayoi period}}, are primarily considered a characteristic of the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}.
Among large Sarcophaguss, there are hollowed-out coffins and assembled ones. Hollowed {{ill|Split-bamboo-shaped Coffins|ja|割竹形石棺|1=Waritakegata Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Split-bamboo-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Sarcophagus|qq=}} and {{ill|Boat-shaped Coffins|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boat-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagata Sarcophagus|qq=}} appeared in the late 4th century.
In the 5th century, {{ill|Long-box-shaped Coffins|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Long-box-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} spread mainly in the central Kinki region, while boat-shaped coffins developed in central Kyushu, {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|WD=Q398310|lt=San'in Region|1=San'in region}}, {{ill|Hokuriku region|en|Hokuriku region|WD=Q381025|lt=Hokuriku Region|1=Hokuriku region}}, and {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō Region|1=Kantō region}}.
By the 6th century, unique local hollowed-out and assembled {{ill|House-shaped Coffins|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} were actively used in parts of {{ill|Kinki|ja|近畿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近畿地方|1=Kansai region|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q164256}}, {{ill|Kyushu|en|Kyushu|WD=Q13987|lt=Kyushu|1=Kyushu}}, {{ill|San'yō region|en|San'yō region|WD=Q398087|lt=San'yō Region|1=San'yō region}}, {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|WD=Q398310|lt=San'in Region|1=San'in region}}, {{ill|Tōkai region|en|Tōkai region|WD=Q398989|lt=Tōkai Region|1=Tōkai region}}, and {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō Region|1=Kantō region}}. Their use continued into the 7th century in some areas. == Stone Coffins in the Jōmon Period == Five Sarcophaguss have been discovered at the Hon-go Yaiseki Ruins in {{ill|Tashiro, Akita|en|Tashiro, Akita|WD=Q7687213|lt=Tashiro, Akita|1=Tashiro, Akita}}, {{ill|Kitaakita District, Akita|en|Kitaakita District, Akita|WD=Q1208682|lt=Kitaakita District, Akita|1=Kitaakita District, Akita}}. Based on the {{ill|Soil Layer|ja|土層 (考古学)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soil Layer|1=Q55524646|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55524646}} containing the coffins and nearby {{ill|Earthenware|en|Earthenware|WD=Q1377111|lt=Pottery|1=Earthenware}}, they are estimated to belong to the late Jōmon period. Six coffins have been found at the Kuharayama-toge Ruins in {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|en|Aomori Prefecture|WD=Q71699|lt=Aomori Prefecture|1=Aomori Prefecture}}.斎藤忠『日本考古学用語辞典 改訂新版』学生社 2004th year 248ページ == Stone Coffins in the Yayoi Period == * '''{{ill|Box-shaped Coffins|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-type Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type Sarcophagus|qq=}}''' were developed. == Stone Coffins in the Kofun Period == * '''{{ill|Split-bamboo-shaped Coffins|ja|割竹形石棺|1=Waritakegata Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Split-bamboo-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Sarcophagus|qq=}}''' (わりだけがたせっかん): Sarcophaguss modeled after split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffins. * '''{{ill|Boat-shaped Coffins|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boat-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagata Sarcophagus|qq=}}''' (ふながたせっかん): Both the lid and body were hollowed out, considered a variation of split-bamboo-shaped coffins. * '''{{ill|Long-box-shaped Coffins|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Long-box-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}''' (ながもちがたせっかん): Measuring 2–3 meters in length, the body was a box-shaped assembly. * '''{{ill|House-shaped Coffins|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}''' (いえがたせっかん): The body was box-shaped, and the lid resembled a roof. == See Also == * practices of the Yayoi period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487466}} * {{ill|Wooden Coffin Tombs in the Yayoi Period|ja|弥生時代の墓制#木棺墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin Tombs in section|section=1|date=2016-03}} * {{ill|Terumi Esaka|ja|江坂輝彌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Terumi Esaka|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江坂輝弥|1=Teruya Esaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11550799}}, {{ill|Chōsuke Serizawa|ja|芹沢長介|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōsuke Serizawa|1=Serizawa Chōsuke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616035}}, and {{ill|Shūichi Sakazume|ja|坂詰秀一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shūichi Sakazume|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 坂誥秀一|1=Hideichi Sakazume|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11425571}}, eds. "Shin Nihon Kōkogaku Kōjiten" (New Japanese Archaeological Dictionary). New Science Press, 2005. ISBN 4-8216-0511-2. * Saitō Tadashi. "Nihon Kōkogaku Yōgo Jiten Kaitei Shinpan" (Dictionary of Japanese Archaeological Terms, Revised Edition). Gakuseisha, 2004. ISBN 978-4-311-75033-5. * Tanaka Taku and Sahara Makoto, eds. "Nihon Kōkogaku Jiten" (Encyclopedia of Japanese Archaeology). Sanseidō, 2003. ISBN 978-4-385-15835-8. * Keiji Nagahara (Supervisor), Eiichi Ishigami, et al. "Iwanami Nihonshi Jiten" (Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary). Iwanami Shoten, 1999. ISBN 978-4-00-080093-8. * ==References== {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|石棺|version=100400894|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:石棺]] [[ja:日本の石棺]] [[en:Sekkan]] {{Commons category|Sarcophaguss}} {{wikidata link|Q11586612}} [[Category:Archaeology of Japan]] [[Category:Coffins]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Stone (material)]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]

[[co:commons:Category:Stone coffins]][[ja:石棺]] [[co:commons:Category:Stone coffins]][[ja:石棺]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Stone coffins]][[ja:石棺]] {{Nihongo|Sekkan|石棺}} refers to a coffin made from stone material.[[File:前塚古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|280px|thumb|{{center|Sarcophagus excavated from {{ill|Maetsuka Kofun|ja|前塚古墳 (高槻市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maetsuka Kofun|qq=}}}}{{small|On display at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|en|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|WD=Q4557237|lt=Chikatsu Asuka Museum|1=Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum}}.}}]] Besides Sarcophaguss, there are also {{ill|Jar_burial|en|Jar_burial|WD=Q4244233|lt=Jar Coffins|1=Jar burial}}, {{ill|Clay Coffins|ja|甕棺|1=Clay coffin chamber|2=ja|3=粘土槨|lt=Clay Coffin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}}, {{ill|Wooden Coffins|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin|1=Wooden Coffin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin|qq=}}, {{ill|Ceramic Coffins|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceramic Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceramic Coffins|qq=}}, and {{ill|Lacquered Coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Dry-lacquer coffin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lacquered Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Dry-lacquer coffin|qq=}}.
Among them, Sarcophaguss are the most durable and airtight, making them suitable for preserving remains. They were crafted and used during the {{ill|Jōmon period|en|Jōmon period|WD=Q52824|lt=Jōmon period|1=Jōmon period}}, {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period|1=Yayoi period}}, and {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}. Sarcophaguss can be classified into smaller box-shaped coffins made by assembling natural flat stones and larger coffins crafted from stone materials.
Box-shaped coffins were also seen on the continent, spreading to eastern Japan in the later {{ill|Jōmon period|en|Jōmon period|WD=Q52824|lt=Jōmon period|1=Jōmon period}} and arriving in northern Kyushu with {{ill|Rice Cultivation|ja|稲作|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rice Cultivation|qq=}} in the early {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period|1=Yayoi period}}.
Large Sarcophaguss, though sporadically seen in the {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period|1=Yayoi period}}, are primarily considered a characteristic of the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}.
Among large Sarcophaguss, there are hollowed-out coffins and assembled ones. Hollowed {{ill|Split-bamboo-shaped Coffins|ja|割竹形石棺|1=Waritakegata Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Split-bamboo-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Sarcophagus|qq=}} and {{ill|Boat-shaped Coffins|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boat-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagata Sarcophagus|qq=}} appeared in the late 4th century.
In the 5th century, {{ill|Long-box-shaped Coffins|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Long-box-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} spread mainly in the central Kinki region, while boat-shaped coffins developed in central Kyushu, {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|WD=Q398310|lt=San'in Region|1=San'in region}}, {{ill|Hokuriku region|en|Hokuriku region|WD=Q381025|lt=Hokuriku Region|1=Hokuriku region}}, and {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō Region|1=Kantō region}}.
By the 6th century, unique local hollowed-out and assembled {{ill|House-shaped Coffins|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} were actively used in parts of {{ill|Kinki|ja|近畿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近畿地方|1=Kansai region|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q164256}}, {{ill|Kyushu|en|Kyushu|WD=Q13987|lt=Kyushu|1=Kyushu}}, {{ill|San'yō region|en|San'yō region|WD=Q398087|lt=San'yō Region|1=San'yō region}}, {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|WD=Q398310|lt=San'in Region|1=San'in region}}, {{ill|Tōkai region|en|Tōkai region|WD=Q398989|lt=Tōkai Region|1=Tōkai region}}, and {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō Region|1=Kantō region}}. Their use continued into the 7th century in some areas. == Stone Coffins in the Jōmon Period == Five Sarcophaguss have been discovered at the Hon-go Yaiseki Ruins in {{ill|Tashiro, Akita|en|Tashiro, Akita|WD=Q7687213|lt=Tashiro, Akita|1=Tashiro, Akita}}, {{ill|Kitaakita District, Akita|en|Kitaakita District, Akita|WD=Q1208682|lt=Kitaakita District, Akita|1=Kitaakita District, Akita}}. Based on the {{ill|Soil Layer|ja|土層 (考古学)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soil Layer|1=Q55524646|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55524646}} containing the coffins and nearby {{ill|Earthenware|en|Earthenware|WD=Q1377111|lt=Pottery|1=Earthenware}}, they are estimated to belong to the late Jōmon period. Six coffins have been found at the Kuharayama-toge Ruins in {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|en|Aomori Prefecture|WD=Q71699|lt=Aomori Prefecture|1=Aomori Prefecture}}.斎藤忠『日本考古学用語辞典 改訂新版』学生社 2004th year 248ページ == Stone Coffins in the Yayoi Period == * '''{{ill|Box-shaped Coffins|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-type Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type Sarcophagus|qq=}}''' were developed. == Stone Coffins in the Kofun Period == * '''{{ill|Split-bamboo-shaped Coffins|ja|割竹形石棺|1=Waritakegata Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Split-bamboo-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Sarcophagus|qq=}}''' (わりだけがたせっかん): Sarcophaguss modeled after split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffins. * '''{{ill|Boat-shaped Coffins|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boat-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagata Sarcophagus|qq=}}''' (ふながたせっかん): Both the lid and body were hollowed out, considered a variation of split-bamboo-shaped coffins. * '''{{ill|Long-box-shaped Coffins|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Long-box-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}''' (ながもちがたせっかん): Measuring 2–3 meters in length, the body was a box-shaped assembly. * '''{{ill|House-shaped Coffins|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}''' (いえがたせっかん): The body was box-shaped, and the lid resembled a roof. == See Also == * {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} * {{ill|Stone Coffin Tombs in the Yayoi Period|ja|弥生時代の墓制#石棺墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone Coffin Tombs in the Yayoi Period|1=Funerary practices of the Yayoi period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487466}} * {{ill|Wooden Coffin Tombs in the Yayoi Period|ja|弥生時代の墓制#木棺墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin Tombs in the Yayoi Period|1=Funerary practices of the Yayoi period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487466}} * {{ill|Stone Coffin Chambers|ja|石棺|1=Sekkan|1=Sekkan (Sarcophagus)|lt=Sarcophagus|qq=式石室|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone Coffin Chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekkan|qq=}} == References == {{Unreferenced section|section=1|date=2016-03}} * {{ill|Terumi Esaka|ja|江坂輝彌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Terumi Esaka|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江坂輝弥|1=Teruya Esaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11550799}}, {{ill|Chōsuke Serizawa|ja|芹沢長介|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōsuke Serizawa|1=Serizawa Chōsuke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616035}}, and {{ill|Shūichi Sakazume|ja|坂詰秀一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shūichi Sakazume|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 坂誥秀一|1=Hideichi Sakazume|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11425571}}, eds. "Shin Nihon Kōkogaku Kōjiten" (New Japanese Archaeological Dictionary). New Science Press, 2005. ISBN 4-8216-0511-2. * Saitō Tadashi. "Nihon Kōkogaku Yōgo Jiten Kaitei Shinpan" (Dictionary of Japanese Archaeological Terms, Revised Edition). Gakuseisha, 2004. ISBN 978-4-311-75033-5. * Tanaka Taku and Sahara Makoto, eds. "Nihon Kōkogaku Jiten" (Encyclopedia of Japanese Archaeology). Sanseidō, 2003. ISBN 978-4-385-15835-8. * Keiji Nagahara (Supervisor), Eiichi Ishigami, et al. "Iwanami Nihonshi Jiten" (Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary). Iwanami Shoten, 1999. ISBN 978-4-00-080093-8. * ==References== {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|石棺|version=100400894|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:石棺]] [[ja:日本の石棺]] [[en:Sekkan]] {{Commons category|Sarcophaguss}} {{wikidata link|Q11586612}} [[Category:Archaeology of Japan]] [[Category:Coffins]] [[Category:Stone (material)]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]

[[co:commons:Category:Stone coffins]][[ja:石棺]] [[co:commons:Category:Stone coffins]][[ja:石棺]] [[ja:石棺]] {{Nihongo|Sekkan|石棺}} refers to a coffin made from stone material.[[File:前塚古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|280px|thumb|{{center|Sarcophagus excavated from {{ill|Maetsuka Kofun|ja|前塚古墳 (高槻市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maetsuka Kofun|qq=}}}}{{small|On display at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|en|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|WD=Q4557237|lt=Chikatsu Asuka Museum|1=Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum}}.}}]] Besides Sarcophaguss, there are also {{ill|Jar_burial|en|Jar_burial|WD=Q4244233|lt=Jar Coffins|1=Jar burial}}, {{ill|Clay Coffins|ja|甕棺|1=Clay coffin chamber|2=ja|3=粘土槨|lt=Clay Coffin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}}, {{ill|Wooden Coffins|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin|1=Wooden Coffin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin|qq=}}, {{ill|Ceramic Coffins|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceramic Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceramic Coffins|qq=}}, and {{ill|Lacquered Coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Dry-lacquer coffin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lacquered Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Dry-lacquer coffin|qq=}}.
Among them, Sarcophaguss are the most durable and airtight, making them suitable for preserving remains. They were crafted and used during the {{ill|Jōmon period|en|Jōmon period|WD=Q52824|lt=Jōmon period|1=Jōmon period}}, {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period|1=Yayoi period}}, and {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}. Sarcophaguss can be classified into smaller box-shaped coffins made by assembling natural flat stones and larger coffins crafted from stone materials.
Box-shaped coffins were also seen on the continent, spreading to eastern Japan in the later {{ill|Jōmon period|en|Jōmon period|WD=Q52824|lt=Jōmon period|1=Jōmon period}} and arriving in northern Kyushu with {{ill|Rice Cultivation|ja|稲作|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rice Cultivation|qq=}} in the early {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period|1=Yayoi period}}.
Large Sarcophaguss, though sporadically seen in the {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period|1=Yayoi period}}, are primarily considered a characteristic of the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}.
Among large Sarcophaguss, there are hollowed-out coffins and assembled ones. Hollowed {{ill|Split-bamboo-shaped Coffins|ja|割竹形石棺|1=Waritakegata Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Split-bamboo-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Sarcophagus|qq=}} and {{ill|Boat-shaped Coffins|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boat-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagata Sarcophagus|qq=}} appeared in the late 4th century.
In the 5th century, {{ill|Long-box-shaped Coffins|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Long-box-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} spread mainly in the central Kinki region, while boat-shaped coffins developed in central Kyushu, {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|WD=Q398310|lt=San'in Region|1=San'in region}}, {{ill|Hokuriku region|en|Hokuriku region|WD=Q381025|lt=Hokuriku Region|1=Hokuriku region}}, and {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō Region|1=Kantō region}}.
By the 6th century, unique local hollowed-out and assembled {{ill|House-shaped Coffins|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} were actively used in parts of {{ill|Kinki|ja|近畿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近畿地方|1=Kansai region|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q164256}}, {{ill|Kyushu|en|Kyushu|WD=Q13987|lt=Kyushu|1=Kyushu}}, {{ill|San'yō region|en|San'yō region|WD=Q398087|lt=San'yō Region|1=San'yō region}}, {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|WD=Q398310|lt=San'in Region|1=San'in region}}, {{ill|Tōkai region|en|Tōkai region|WD=Q398989|lt=Tōkai Region|1=Tōkai region}}, and {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō Region|1=Kantō region}}. Their use continued into the 7th century in some areas. == Stone Coffins in the Jōmon Period == Five Sarcophaguss have been discovered at the Hon-go Yaiseki Ruins in {{ill|Tashiro, Akita|en|Tashiro, Akita|WD=Q7687213|lt=Tashiro, Akita|1=Tashiro, Akita}}, {{ill|Kitaakita District, Akita|en|Kitaakita District, Akita|WD=Q1208682|lt=Kitaakita District, Akita|1=Kitaakita District, Akita}}. Based on the {{ill|Soil Layer|ja|土層 (考古学)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soil Layer|1=Q55524646|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55524646}} containing the coffins and nearby {{ill|Earthenware|en|Earthenware|WD=Q1377111|lt=Pottery|1=Earthenware}}, they are estimated to belong to the late Jōmon period. Six coffins have been found at the Kuharayama-toge Ruins in {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|en|Aomori Prefecture|WD=Q71699|lt=Aomori Prefecture|1=Aomori Prefecture}}.斎藤忠『日本考古学用語辞典 改訂新版』学生社 2004th year 248ページ == Stone Coffins in the Yayoi Period == * '''{{ill|Box-shaped Coffins|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-type Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type Sarcophagus|qq=}}''' were developed. == Stone Coffins in the Kofun Period == * '''{{ill|Split-bamboo-shaped Coffins|ja|割竹形石棺|1=Waritakegata Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Split-bamboo-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Sarcophagus|qq=}}''' (わりだけがたせっかん): Sarcophaguss modeled after split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffins. * '''{{ill|Boat-shaped Coffins|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boat-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagata Sarcophagus|qq=}}''' (ふながたせっかん): Both the lid and body were hollowed out, considered a variation of split-bamboo-shaped coffins. * '''{{ill|Long-box-shaped Coffins|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Long-box-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}''' (ながもちがたせっかん): Measuring 2–3 meters in length, the body was a box-shaped assembly. * '''{{ill|House-shaped Coffins|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}''' (いえがたせっかん): The body was box-shaped, and the lid resembled a roof. == See Also == * {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} * {{ill|Stone Coffin Tombs in the Yayoi Period|ja|弥生時代の墓制#石棺墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone Coffin Tombs in the Yayoi Period|1=Funerary practices of the Yayoi period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487466}} * {{ill|Wooden Coffin Tombs in the Yayoi Period|ja|弥生時代の墓制#木棺墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin Tombs in the Yayoi Period|1=Funerary practices of the Yayoi period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487466}} * {{ill|Stone Coffin Chambers|ja|石棺|1=Sekkan|1=Sekkan (Sarcophagus)|lt=Sarcophagus|qq=式石室|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone Coffin Chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekkan|qq=}} == References == {{Unreferenced Sakazume|ja|坂詰秀一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shūichi Sakazume|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 坂誥秀一|1=Hideichi Sakazume|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11425571}}, eds.
